title: Davilla aspera
taxonomy: Davilla aspera is classified within the family Opiliaceae. This plant belongs to the order Santalales. Davilla aspera is further categorized as a species within the genus Davilla. The classification of Davilla aspera reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in the same family and order. Its classification also aids in the identification and study of the plant's unique features and properties.
uses: Davilla aspera is a plant species known for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, it has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever and inflammation. The plant's leaves and roots are often employed in folk medicine to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. In addition, Davilla aspera is believed to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential applications.

constituents: Davilla aspera contains alkaloids, particularly davilla alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The davilla alkaloids have been reported to possess various bioactive properties, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. intro: Davilla aspera is a medicinal plant species in the family Opiliaceae, commonly known for its traditional use in folk medicine. The health benefits of D. aspera include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been utilized to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of D. aspera include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, particularly davilline and davillaline, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with D. aspera include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health conditions and promote overall well-being.
